Preparation¶

import torch

from torchvision import datasets, transforms
from torchmetrics.image.inception import InceptionScore
from torchmetrics.image.fid import FrechetInceptionDistance

GPU check¶

This section is mainly focused on the device we are about to use and does not interfere with other parts and can be just replaced with the following code:

device = torch.device("cpu")

In [40]:
if torch.cuda.is_available():
    device = torch.device("cuda")
    print("Using GPU:", torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))
else:
    device = torch.device("cpu")
    print("Using CPU")

Using GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU

Constants¶

In [41]:
LEARNING_RATE = 0.001
BATCH_SIZE = 128
NUM_EPOCHS = 50
RANDOM_SEED = 42

Randomization control¶

In [42]:
torch.manual_seed(RANDOM_SEED)
np.random.seed(RANDOM_SEED)
random.seed(RANDOM_SEED)
torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(RANDOM_SEED)

Loading Datasets¶

MNIST¶

In [43]:
class MNIST_DATA:
    def __init__(self):
        self.transform = transforms.Compose([
            transforms.ToTensor(),
            transforms.Normalize((0.5,), (0.5,))
        ])

        train_val_dataset = datasets.MNIST(root="./Data", train=True, download=True, transform=self.transform)
        self.test_dataset = datasets.MNIST(root="./Data", train=False, download=True, transform=self.transform)
        self.train_dataset, self.val_dataset = random_split(train_val_dataset, [50000, 10000])
        self.class_names = [str(i) for i in range(10)]

    def get_loaders(self, batch_size=32):
        train_loader = DataLoader(self.train_dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=True)
        val_loader = DataLoader(self.val_dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=False)
        test_loader = DataLoader(self.test_dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=False)
        return train_loader, val_loader, test_loader

    def get_class_names(self):
        return {i: name for i, name in enumerate(self.class_names)}

SVHN¶

In [44]:
class SVHN_DATA:
    def __init__(self):
        self.transform = transforms.Compose([
            transforms.ToTensor(),
            transforms.Normalize((0.5, 0.5, 0.5),
                                 (0.5, 0.5, 0.5))
        ])

        train_val_dataset = datasets.SVHN(root="./Data/SVHN", split="train", download=True, transform=self.transform)
        self.test_dataset = datasets.SVHN(root="./Data/SVHN", split="test", download=True, transform=self.transform)
        self.train_dataset, self.val_dataset = random_split(train_val_dataset, [60000, 13257])
        self.class_names = [str(i) for i in range(10)]

    def get_loaders(self, batch_size=32):
        train_loader = DataLoader(self.train_dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=True)
        val_loader = DataLoader(self.val_dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=False)
        test_loader = DataLoader(self.test_dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=False)
        return train_loader, val_loader, test_loader

    def get_class_names(self):
        return {i: name for i, name in enumerate(self.class_names)}

CelebA¶

In [45]:
class CelebA_DATA:
    def __init__(self):
        self.transform = transforms.Compose([
            transforms.Resize((64, 64)),
            transforms.ToTensor(),
            transforms.Normalize((0.5, 0.5, 0.5),
                                 (0.5, 0.5, 0.5)),
            transforms.Resize((32,32))
        ])

        self.train_dataset = datasets.CelebA(root="./Data", split="train", target_type="attr", download=True, transform=self.transform)
        self.val_dataset = datasets.CelebA(root="./Data", split="valid", target_type="attr", download=True, transform=self.transform)
        self.test_dataset = datasets.CelebA(root="./Data", split="test", target_type="attr", download=True, transform=self.transform)
        self.class_names = self.train_dataset.attr_names

    def get_loaders(self, batch_size=32):
        train_loader = DataLoader(self.train_dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=True)
        val_loader = DataLoader(self.val_dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=False)
        test_loader = DataLoader(self.test_dataset, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=False)
        return train_loader, val_loader, test_loader

    def get_class_names(self):
        return {i: name for i, name in enumerate(self.class_names)}

Visualization¶

In [46]:
def show_batch(loader, class_names=None, n_cols=8, show_label=False, max_attrs=3):
    images, labels = next(iter(loader))
    batch_size = len(images)
    n_rows = math.ceil(batch_size / n_cols)

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(n_rows, n_cols, figsize=(2 * n_cols, 2 * n_rows), squeeze=False,)

    axes = axes.ravel()
    for ax in axes:
        ax.axis("off")
    for i, (img, label) in enumerate(zip(images, labels)):
        img = img * 0.5 + 0.5
        img = img.clamp(0, 1)
        if img.shape[0] == 1:
            axes[i].imshow(img.squeeze(), cmap="gray")
        else:
            axes[i].imshow(img.permute(1, 2, 0))
        if label.ndim == 0:
            label = label.item()
            title = class_names[label] if class_names is not None else str(label)
        else:
            active = torch.where(label == 1)[0].tolist()
            if class_names is not None:
                attrs = [class_names[j] for j in active[:max_attrs]]
            else:
                attrs = [str(j) for j in active[:max_attrs]]
            if len(active) > max_attrs:
                attrs.append("...")
            title = "\n".join(attrs)
        if show_label:
            axes[i].set_title(title, fontsize=10)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

Model Definition¶

Base model¶

In [47]:
class BaseModel(nn.Module, ABC):
    def __init__(self, learning_rate=1e-3, device='cpu', seed=42):
        torch.manual_seed(seed)
        super().__init__()

        self.learning_rate = learning_rate
        self.optimizer = None
        
        self.device = device if isinstance(device, torch.device) else torch.device(device)
        self.to(self.device)

    # --------------------------------------------------------
    # Learning Rate
    # --------------------------------------------------------
    def get_learning_rate(self):
        return self.learning_rate

    def set_learning_rate(self, lr):
        self.learning_rate = lr

        if self.optimizer is not None:
            for group in self.optimizer.param_groups:
                group["lr"] = lr

    # --------------------------------------------------------
    # Device
    # --------------------------------------------------------
    def get_device(self):
        return self.device

    def set_device(self, device):
        self.device = device
        self.to(device)

    # --------------------------------------------------------
    # Freeze
    # --------------------------------------------------------
    def freeze(self):
        for p in self.parameters():
            p.requires_grad = False

    def unfreeze(self):
        for p in self.parameters():
            p.requires_grad = True

    # --------------------------------------------------------
    # Save / Load
    # --------------------------------------------------------
    def save_model(self, path):
        torch.save(self.state_dict(), path)

    def load_model(self, path):
        self.load_state_dict(torch.load(path, map_location=self.device))

    # --------------------------------------------------------
    # Summary
    # --------------------------------------------------------
    def summarize(self):
        print(self)
        total = sum(p.numel() for p in self.parameters())
        trainable = sum(
            p.numel() for p in self.parameters()
            if p.requires_grad
        )

        print(f"\nTotal Parameters     : {total:,}")
        print(f"Trainable Parameters : {trainable:,}")

    # --------------------------------------------------------
    def train_one_epoch(self, *args, **kwargs):
        raise NotImplementedError

Encoder¶

In [48]:
class Encoder(BaseModel):
    def __init__(self, architecture="small", learning_rate=1e-3, device="cpu", seed=42):
        super().__init__(learning_rate, device, seed)
        # --------------------------------------------------
        # Architectures
        # --------------------------------------------------
        model_mnist = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(28 * 28, 512),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
        )

        model_svhn_celeb = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(3, 64, kernel_size=5, stride=2, padding=2),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(64),
            nn.ReLU(True),

            nn.Conv2d(64, 128, kernel_size=5, stride=2, padding=2),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(128),
            nn.ReLU(True),

            nn.Conv2d(128, 256, kernel_size=5, stride=2, padding=2),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(256),
            nn.ReLU(True),

            nn.Flatten(),

            nn.Linear(4 * 4 * 256, 2048),
            nn.BatchNorm1d(2048),
            nn.ReLU(True)
        )

        models = {
            "MNIST": {
                "feature_extractor": model_mnist,
                "mu": nn.Linear(512, 50),
                "logvar": nn.Linear(512, 50),
            },
            "SVHN": {
                "feature_extractor": model_svhn_celeb,
                "mu": nn.Linear(2048, 1024),
                "logvar": nn.Linear(2048, 1024),
            },
            "CELEB": {
                "feature_extractor": model_svhn_celeb,
                "mu": nn.Linear(2048, 2048),
                "logvar": nn.Linear(2048, 2048),
            },
        }

        selected = models[architecture]

        self.feature_extractor = selected["feature_extractor"]
        self.mu = selected["mu"]
        self.logvar = selected["logvar"]

        self.optimizer = optim.Adam(
            self.parameters(),
            lr=self.learning_rate,
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        h = self.feature_extractor(x)
        mu = self.mu(h)
        logvar = self.logvar(h)
        return mu, logvar

    def train_one_epoch(self, dataloader, criterion):
        self.train()
        total_loss = 0
        for x, _ in dataloader:
            x = x.to(self.device)
            self.optimizer.zero_grad()
            mu, logvar = self(x)
            loss = criterion(mu, logvar)
            loss.backward()
            self.optimizer.step()
            total_loss += loss.item()

        return total_loss / len(dataloader)

Decoder¶

In [49]:
class Decoder(BaseModel):
    def __init__(self, architecture="small", learning_rate=1e-3, device="cpu", seed=42):
        super().__init__(learning_rate, device, seed)
        # --------------------------------------------------
        # Architectures
        # --------------------------------------------------
        model_mnist = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(50, 512),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Linear(512, 28 * 28),
            nn.Sigmoid(),
            nn.Unflatten(1, (1, 28, 28))
        )

        model_svhn = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(1024, 8 * 8 * 256),
            nn.BatchNorm1d(8 * 8 * 256),
            nn.ReLU(True),

            nn.Unflatten(1, (256, 8, 8)),

            nn.ConvTranspose2d(256, 256, kernel_size=5, stride=2, padding=2, output_padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(256),
            nn.ReLU(True),
            nn.ConvTranspose2d(256, 128, kernel_size=5, stride=2, padding=2, output_padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(128),
            nn.ReLU(True),
            nn.ConvTranspose2d(128, 32, kernel_size=5, stride=2, padding=2, output_padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(32),
            nn.ReLU(True),
            nn.Conv2d(32, 3, kernel_size=5, padding=2),
            nn.Tanh()
        )

        model_celeb = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(2048, 8 * 8 * 256),
            nn.BatchNorm1d(8 * 8 * 256),
            nn.ReLU(True),

            nn.Unflatten(1, (256, 8, 8)),

            nn.ConvTranspose2d(256, 256, kernel_size=5, stride=2, padding=2, output_padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(256),
            nn.ReLU(True),
            nn.ConvTranspose2d(256, 128, kernel_size=5, stride=2, padding=2, output_padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(128),
            nn.ReLU(True),
            nn.ConvTranspose2d(128, 32, kernel_size=5, stride=2, padding=2, output_padding=1),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(32),
            nn.ReLU(True),
            nn.Conv2d(32, 3, kernel_size=5, padding=2),
            nn.Tanh()
        )
        models = {
            "MNIST": model_mnist,
            "SVHN": model_svhn,
            "CELEB": model_celeb,
        }

        self.decoder = models[architecture]
        self.optimizer = optim.Adam(
            self.parameters(),
            lr=self.learning_rate,
        )

    def forward(self, z):
        return self.decoder(z)

    def train_one_epoch(self, dataloader, criterion):
        self.train()
        total_loss = 0
        for z, target in dataloader:
            z = z.to(self.device)
            target = target.to(self.device)
            self.optimizer.zero_grad()
            output = self(z)
            loss = criterion(output, target)
            loss.backward()
            self.optimizer.step()
            total_loss += loss.item()
        return total_loss / len(dataloader)

Variational auto-encoder¶

In [50]:
class VAE(BaseModel):
    def __init__(self, criterion, learning_rate, architecture="MNIST", device="cpu", seed=42):
        super().__init__(learning_rate, device, seed)

        self.criterion = criterion
        self.architecture = architecture

        self.encoder = Encoder(
            architecture=self.architecture,
            learning_rate=learning_rate,
            device=device,
            seed=seed,
        )
        self.decoder = Decoder(
            architecture=self.architecture,
            learning_rate=learning_rate,
            device=device,
            seed=seed,
        )
        self.optimizer = optim.Adam(
            self.parameters(),
            lr=self.learning_rate,
        )
        self.to(device)

    # -----------------------------------------------------

    def reparameterize(self, mu, logvar):
        std = torch.exp(0.5 * logvar)
        eps = torch.randn_like(std)
        return mu + eps * std

    # -----------------------------------------------------

    def forward(self, x):
        mu, logvar = self.encoder(x)
        z = self.reparameterize(mu, logvar)
        reconstruction = self.decoder(z)
        return reconstruction, mu, logvar

    # -----------------------------------------------------

    def loss_function(self, reconstruction, x, mu, logvar):
        reconstruction_loss = self.criterion(reconstruction, x,)
        kl = -0.5 * torch.sum(1 + logvar - mu.pow(2) - logvar.exp())
        return reconstruction_loss + kl

    # -----------------------------------------------------

    def train_one_epoch(self, train_loader, val_loader):
        # ----- Training -----
        self.train()
        train_loss = 0.0
        for x, _ in train_loader:
            x = x.to(self.device)
            self.optimizer.zero_grad()
            reconstruction, mu, logvar = self(x)
            loss = self.loss_function(reconstruction, x, mu, logvar)
            loss.backward()
            self.optimizer.step()
            train_loss += loss.item()
        train_loss /= len(train_loader)

        # ----- Validation -----
        self.eval()
        val_loss = 0.0
        with torch.no_grad():
            for x, _ in val_loader:
                x = x.to(self.device)
                reconstruction, mu, logvar = self(x)
                loss = self.loss_function(reconstruction, x, mu, logvar)
                val_loss += loss.item()
        val_loss /= len(val_loader)

        return train_loss, val_loss
    
    def save_model(self, base_path):
        self.encoder.save_model(base_path + f'\\{self.architecture}_Encoder')
        self.decoder.save_model(base_path + f'\\{self.architecture}_Decoder')

    def load_model(self, base_path):
        main_path = base_path + f'\\{self.architecture}'
        if Path(main_path + '_Encoder').exists() and Path(main_path + '_Decoder').exists():
            self.encoder.load_model(main_path + '_Encoder')
            self.decoder.load_model(main_path + '_Decoder')
            return True
        else:
            return False

Evaluation¶

In [14]:
def plot_losses(train_losses, val_losses):
    epochs = range(1, len(train_losses) + 1)

    plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
    plt.plot(epochs, train_losses, label="Train Loss", linewidth=2)
    plt.plot(epochs, val_losses, label="Validation Loss", linewidth=2)

    plt.title("Training and Validation Loss")
    plt.xlabel("Epoch")
    plt.ylabel("Loss")
    plt.xticks(epochs)
    plt.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.6)
    plt.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
In [15]:
def show_reconstructions(model, dataloader, num_images=-1, n_cols=16):
    model.eval()

    images, _ = next(iter(dataloader))
    if num_images == -1:
        num_images = len(images)

    if num_images > len(images):
        raise ValueError(
            f"num_images ({num_images}) exceeds batch size ({len(images)})."
        )

    images = images[:num_images].to(model.device)
    with torch.no_grad():
        reconstructions, _, _ = model(images)

    images = images.cpu()
    reconstructions = reconstructions.cpu()
    if n_cols == -1:
        n_cols = num_images

    n_rows = math.ceil(num_images / n_cols)

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(2 * n_rows, n_cols, figsize=(2 * n_cols, 4 * n_rows), squeeze=False)

    for ax in axes.ravel():
        ax.axis("off")

    for i in range(num_images):
        row = (i // n_cols) * 2
        col = i % n_cols

        img = images[i].permute(1, 2, 0).squeeze()
        axes[row, col].imshow(img, cmap="gray")
        axes[row, col].set_title("Original", fontsize=10)

        rec = reconstructions[i].permute(1, 2, 0).squeeze()
        axes[row + 1, col].imshow(rec, cmap="gray")
        axes[row + 1, col].set_title("Reconstruction", fontsize=10)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

Training VAEs¶

In [30]:
mnist = MNIST_DATA()
mnist_train, mnist_val, _ = mnist.get_loaders(batch_size=BATCH_SIZE)
show_batch(mnist_train, class_names=mnist.get_class_names(), n_cols=16)
In [33]:
train_loss = []
val_loss   = []
final_acc = {'Train': [], 'Validation': [], 'Test': []}
path = f'.\\Models\\V{NUM_EPOCHS}'
vae_model = VAE(
    criterion=nn.MSELoss(reduction="sum"),
    learning_rate=LEARNING_RATE,
    architecture="MNIST",
    device=device,
    seed=42
)
vae_model.summarize()
if not vae_model.load_model(path):
    for epoch in tqdm(range(NUM_EPOCHS)):
        t_loss, v_loss = vae_model.train_one_epoch(mnist_train, mnist_val)
        train_loss.append(t_loss)
        val_loss.append(v_loss)

    vae_model.save_model(path)


plot_losses(train_loss, val_loss)
show_reconstructions(vae_model, mnist_val, -1)

VAE(
  (criterion): MSELoss()
  (encoder): Encoder(
    (feature_extractor): Sequential(
      (0): Flatten(start_dim=1, end_dim=-1)
      (1): Linear(in_features=784, out_features=512, bias=True)
      (2): ReLU(inplace=True)
    )
    (mu): Linear(in_features=512, out_features=50, bias=True)
    (logvar): Linear(in_features=512, out_features=50, bias=True)
  )
  (decoder): Decoder(
    (decoder): Sequential(
      (0): Linear(in_features=50, out_features=512, bias=True)
      (1): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (2): Linear(in_features=512, out_features=784, bias=True)
      (3): Sigmoid()
      (4): Unflatten(dim=1, unflattened_size=(1, 28, 28))
    )
  )
)

In [51]:
svhn = SVHN_DATA()
svhn_train, svhn_val, _ = svhn.get_loaders(batch_size=BATCH_SIZE)
show_batch(svhn_train, class_names=svhn.get_class_names(), n_cols=16)
In [52]:
train_loss = []
val_loss   = []
final_acc = {'Train': [], 'Validation': [], 'Test': []}
path = f'.\\Models\\V{NUM_EPOCHS}'
vae_model = VAE(
    criterion=nn.MSELoss(reduction="sum"),
    learning_rate=LEARNING_RATE,
    architecture="SVHN",
    device=device,
    seed=42
)
vae_model.summarize()
if not vae_model.load_model(path):
    for epoch in tqdm(range(NUM_EPOCHS)):
        t_loss, v_loss = vae_model.train_one_epoch(svhn_train, svhn_val)
        train_loss.append(t_loss)
        val_loss.append(v_loss)

    vae_model.save_model(path)


plot_losses(train_loss, val_loss)
show_reconstructions(vae_model, svhn_val, -1)

VAE(
  (criterion): MSELoss()
  (encoder): Encoder(
    (feature_extractor): Sequential(
      (0): Conv2d(3, 64, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(2, 2), padding=(2, 2))
      (1): BatchNorm2d(64, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (2): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (3): Conv2d(64, 128, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(2, 2), padding=(2, 2))
      (4): BatchNorm2d(128, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (5): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (6): Conv2d(128, 256, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(2, 2), padding=(2, 2))
      (7): BatchNorm2d(256, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (8): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (9): Flatten(start_dim=1, end_dim=-1)
      (10): Linear(in_features=4096, out_features=2048, bias=True)
      (11): BatchNorm1d(2048, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (12): ReLU(inplace=True)
    )
    (mu): Linear(in_features=2048, out_features=1024, bias=True)
    (logvar): Linear(in_features=2048, out_features=1024, bias=True)
  )
  (decoder): Decoder(
    (decoder): Sequential(
      (0): Linear(in_features=1024, out_features=16384, bias=True)
      (1): BatchNorm1d(16384, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (2): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (3): Unflatten(dim=1, unflattened_size=(256, 8, 8))
      (4): ConvTranspose2d(256, 256, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(2, 2), padding=(2, 2), output_padding=(1, 1))
      (5): BatchNorm2d(256, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (6): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (7): ConvTranspose2d(256, 128, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(2, 2), padding=(2, 2), output_padding=(1, 1))
      (8): BatchNorm2d(128, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (9): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (10): ConvTranspose2d(128, 32, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(2, 2), padding=(2, 2), output_padding=(1, 1))
      (11): BatchNorm2d(32, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (12): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (13): Conv2d(32, 3, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(1, 1), padding=(2, 2))
      (14): Tanh()
    )
  )
)

In [54]:
train_loss = []
val_loss   = []
final_acc = {'Train': [], 'Validation': [], 'Test': []}
path = f'.\\Models\\V{NUM_EPOCHS}'
vae_model = VAE(
    criterion=nn.MSELoss(reduction="sum"),
    learning_rate=LEARNING_RATE,
    architecture="CELEB",
    device=device,
    seed=42
)
vae_model.summarize()
if not vae_model.load_model(path):
    for epoch in tqdm(range(NUM_EPOCHS)):
        t_loss, v_loss = vae_model.train_one_epoch(celeb_train, celeb_val)
        train_loss.append(t_loss)
        val_loss.append(v_loss)

    vae_model.save_model(path)


plot_losses(train_loss, val_loss)
show_reconstructions(vae_model, celeb_val, -1)

VAE(
  (criterion): MSELoss()
  (encoder): Encoder(
    (feature_extractor): Sequential(
      (0): Conv2d(3, 64, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(2, 2), padding=(2, 2))
      (1): BatchNorm2d(64, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (2): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (3): Conv2d(64, 128, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(2, 2), padding=(2, 2))
      (4): BatchNorm2d(128, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (5): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (6): Conv2d(128, 256, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(2, 2), padding=(2, 2))
      (7): BatchNorm2d(256, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (8): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (9): Flatten(start_dim=1, end_dim=-1)
      (10): Linear(in_features=4096, out_features=2048, bias=True)
      (11): BatchNorm1d(2048, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (12): ReLU(inplace=True)
    )
    (mu): Linear(in_features=2048, out_features=2048, bias=True)
    (logvar): Linear(in_features=2048, out_features=2048, bias=True)
  )
  (decoder): Decoder(
    (decoder): Sequential(
      (0): Linear(in_features=2048, out_features=16384, bias=True)
      (1): BatchNorm1d(16384, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (2): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (3): Unflatten(dim=1, unflattened_size=(256, 8, 8))
      (4): ConvTranspose2d(256, 256, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(2, 2), padding=(2, 2), output_padding=(1, 1))
      (5): BatchNorm2d(256, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (6): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (7): ConvTranspose2d(256, 128, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(2, 2), padding=(2, 2), output_padding=(1, 1))
      (8): BatchNorm2d(128, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (9): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (10): ConvTranspose2d(128, 32, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(2, 2), padding=(2, 2), output_padding=(1, 1))
      (11): BatchNorm2d(32, eps=1e-05, momentum=0.1, affine=True, track_running_stats=True)
      (12): ReLU(inplace=True)
      (13): Conv2d(32, 3, kernel_size=(5, 5), stride=(1, 1), padding=(2, 2))
      (14): Tanh()
    )
  )
)
